product_overview: PolyDub is a multilingual communication app that lets users host live translated broadcasts, join language-specific listener pages, create or join real-time multilingual rooms, and upload videos for automated dubbing and subtitle export.
core_goals: Start and share a live multilingual broadcast from a local microphone (optionally with webcam) to listeners in one or more target languages.
Join a language-specific listener view to hear dubbed audio and follow live transcripts.
Create or join real-time rooms for multilingual conversation with translated audio and transcripts.
Upload a video, select languages and voices, and generate dubbed video and subtitle outputs for download.

features.name: Landing Navigation
features.description: Discover the product, change interface locale, and move into core flows (Broadcast, Rooms, VOD).
user_flows: Open / -> Read product overview -> Switch the interface language -> Navigate to /broadcast -> See broadcast setup options
Open / -> Read product overview -> Switch the interface language -> Navigate to /rooms -> See room lobby and options
Open / -> Read product overview -> Switch the interface language -> Navigate to /vod -> See video dubbing studio

features.name: Live Broadcast Hosting
features.description: Configure source and target languages, enable microphone and optional webcam, then start a live translated broadcast and obtain a listener link to share.
user_flows: Open /broadcast -> Choose a source language -> Select one or more target languages -> Allow microphone access -> Optionally enable webcam streaming -> Start broadcasting -> Copy or share the generated listener link
Open /broadcast -> Select source language -> Attempt to start broadcast without selecting any target language -> See validation prompting to select at least one target language

features.name: Broadcast Listening
features.description: Open a listener view for a chosen language to hear translated audio, follow live transcripts, and optionally view incoming webcam video.
user_flows: Open a shared listener URL for a selected language -> Allow or start audio playback -> Hear dubbed audio in the selected language -> See live transcript updates -> View incoming webcam video if available
Open a shared listener URL for a selected language -> Deny or mute audio playback -> Observe muted audio state and a prompt to enable audio to hear the broadcast

features.name: Multilingual Rooms
features.description: Create or join a room to configure languages and voices, use microphone and optional camera, and participate in real-time translated conversations with transcripts.
user_flows: Open /rooms -> Create a new room -> Choose source language, target language(s), and preferred voice -> Allow microphone access -> Optionally enable camera -> Speak and receive translated audio and live transcripts
Open /rooms -> Enter an existing room ID to join -> Choose or confirm preferred target language and voice -> Allow microphone access -> Hear translated audio and view live transcripts
Open /rooms -> Enter an invalid or malformed room ID -> See validation error prompting correct room ID format

features.name: Video Dubbing Studio (VOD)
features.description: Upload a supported video, choose source and target languages and voices, run the dubbing pipeline, preview results, and export dubbed video or subtitle files.
user_flows: Open /vod -> Upload a supported video file -> Select source language, target language(s), and voice options -> Start the dubbing pipeline -> Preview the generated dubbed video and subtitles -> Export subtitle or video output
Open /vod -> Attempt to upload an unsupported file type -> See validation error indicating unsupported file type

routes.description: Landing page with navigation to broadcast, room, and VOD experiences.
Host setup flow for starting a live multilingual broadcast.
Listener page for a selected target language during a live broadcast.
Room lobby for creating a room or joining an existing room by ID.
Real-time multilingual room with audio, optional video, and participant controls.
Video dubbing studio for uploading media, selecting languages, and exporting outputs.

features.name: Landing Navigation
features.description: Users can navigate from the landing page into the core product flows and switch interface locale.
user_interactions: Click into Broadcast, Rooms, or VOD flows.

features.name: Live Broadcast Hosting
features.description: A broadcaster selects source and target languages, uses microphone and optional webcam, and starts a live translated stream.
user_interactions: Start broadcasting and share the listener link.

features.name: Broadcast Listening
features.description: Listeners join a language-specific broadcast page to hear dubbed audio and view transcripts.
user_interactions: Open a shared listener URL for a selected language.
View optional incoming webcam video if available.

features.name: Multilingual Rooms
features.description: Users create or join rooms, configure language and voice preferences, and communicate in real time.
user_interactions: Create a new room or enter an existing room ID.
Choose source language, target language, and preferred voice.
Allow microphone access and optionally enable camera.
Speak and receive translated audio and transcripts in the room.

features.name: Video Dubbing Studio
features.description: Users upload a supported media file, choose output languages and voices, then generate dubbed video and subtitle output.
user_interactions: Select source and target languages and voice options.
Preview the result and export subtitle or video output.

known_limitations.issue: Core live translation features depend on a separate WebSocket server and external Deepgram/Lingo credentials.
known_limitations.impact: End-to-end live audio and translation scenarios may fail unless the local WebSocket service and API keys are configured.

known_limitations.issue: Video dubbing relies on backend API routes and media-processing dependencies.
known_limitations.impact: Upload and dubbing tests may fail or be partial in environments without required API keys or ffmpeg-compatible setup.
